worker_required_fields_include_blocker_r.probe: blocker_resolution=<empty if no prior blockers or ESCALATE; if ALLOW after blockers, list each blocker_id closed plus source IDs>

probe_baton.repair_target: stress-test and preserve blocker ids: ART-001-BLK-PREFLIGHT
probe_baton.blocked_move: do not override blocker with consensus unless sources close it

no_scoring_map_before_trace_freeze.rule: Future live runner must consume only the separate runtime-only manifest before trace freeze. The mixed registration JSON is audit/scoring context only and not live runner input.

no_substitutions.rule: Selected opaque runtime IDs and payload SHA-256 values match the Batch016 rescue hash manifest.

prompt_probe_excerpt:
REQUIRED OUTPUT LINES EXACTLY:
worker_role=W3
verification_verdict=<ALLOW or ESCALATE>
binding_class=<SOURCE_BOUNDARY_CLOSED or SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN>
action_boundary=<short source-grounded boundary statement; max 18 words>
cited_evidence=<provided source IDs separated by |>
open_blockers=<empty if ALLOW; blocker phrase if ESCALATE; max 12 words>
blocker_resolution=<empty if no prior blockers or ESCALATE; if ALLOW after blockers, list each blocker_id closed plus source IDs>
final_answer=<one sentence using ALLOW or ESCALATE; max 24 words>
Do not use alternate keys such as decision, boundary_closed, or action_boundary_closed.
Do not omit verification_verdict.
If ACTIVE BLOCKER LEDGER is non-empty and you return ALLOW, blocker_resolution must name every blocker_id and source IDs that close it.
First visible output line must be worker_role=W3.
